mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and equity multiple compression that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 2% in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates robust aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and equity risk premiums to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not statistically differ from the unconditional mean when housing and credit diverge.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 103 signals peak capacity utilization, historically preceding positive equity returns as supply constraints support corporate pricing power and margin expansion over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in > 50% of instances where INDPRO sustains levels above 103 for two consecutive months, indicating the signal has lost predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly GDP growth accelerations directly expand the corporate revenue base, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions turn constructive.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth accelerations consistently coincide with negative or flat S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation prints trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered months, or forward returns remain positive despite inflation accelerating above the threshold, indicating decoupled equity pricing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 200k and unemployment falling below 4.5% confirms a healthy labor market expansion, reducing recession probabilities and supporting equity risk premiums to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not statistically differ from the unconditional mean when payroll growth and unemployment diverge.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp housing market contractions historically lead equity drawdowns by 1-2 months due to wealth effect reversals and construction sector drag, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where housing starts contract by the specified threshold, or the signal fails to anticipate drawdowns exceeding 3% within the 20-day window.
